focus: premium faceless short-form Java educational reels
audience: aspiring and working developers learning Java, beginner through advanced
format: vertical short-form video (reel), one topic per reel

topics.title: What Is Java
topics.learning_objective: Explain what Java is and why it's used (platform independence, 'write once run anywhere').
common_mistakes: Confusing Java the language with the JVM platform
Thinking Java is interpreted only, with no compilation step
example_ideas: Compile once, run the same .class file on two different OS screenshots
One-line history + why bytecode matters

topics.title: JDK vs JRE vs JVM
topics.learning_objective: Distinguish the JDK, JRE, and JVM and what each is responsible for.
common_mistakes: Using JDK/JRE/JVM interchangeably
Believing you need the full JDK just to run a compiled program
example_ideas: Diagram: JDK contains JRE contains JVM
Which one do you need to just run vs. to develop?

topics.title: Variables and Data Types
topics.learning_objective: Declare variables using Java's primitive types and understand the difference between primitives and objects.
common_mistakes: Forgetting Java is statically typed and mixing incompatible types
Confusing int overflow behavior
example_ideas: The 8 primitive types in one visual
int vs double vs the wrapper class Integer

topics.title: Operators
topics.learning_objective: Use arithmetic, relational, and logical operators correctly, including operator precedence.
common_mistakes: Confusing = with ==
Getting bitten by integer division truncation
example_ideas: 5 / 2 vs 5.0 / 2 side by side
== vs .equals() teaser (full topic comes later)

topics.title: Type Casting
topics.learning_objective: Convert between compatible types explicitly and implicitly, and recognize when data can be lost.
common_mistakes: Casting a large type to a smaller one without realizing data truncates
Not knowing when a cast is required vs automatic
example_ideas: double to int truncation in one line
Widening vs narrowing cast side by side

topics.title: If / Else Statements
topics.learning_objective: Branch program logic using if, else if, and else.
common_mistakes: Missing braces leading to dangling-else confusion
Chaining redundant else-if checks that could be simplified
example_ideas: Grade calculator in 4 lines
Common dangling-else bug

topics.title: Switch Statements
topics.learning_objective: Replace long if-else chains with switch, including fall-through behavior.
common_mistakes: Forgetting break and causing accidental fall-through
Not covering the default case
example_ideas: Day-of-week switch with intentional fall-through demo
Old switch vs switch expression teaser

topics.title: The Ternary Operator
topics.learning_objective: Write concise conditional expressions using the ternary operator without sacrificing readability.
common_mistakes: Nesting ternaries until they're unreadable
Using a ternary for a statement instead of an expression
example_ideas: if-else vs ternary rewrite side by side
When a ternary hurts readability

topics.title: For Loops
topics.learning_objective: Iterate a known number of times using a for loop, including the enhanced for-each form.
common_mistakes: Off-by-one errors in loop bounds
Modifying the loop variable inside the body unexpectedly
example_ideas: Classic for loop vs for-each side by side
Off-by-one bug caught live

topics.title: While Loops
topics.learning_objective: Iterate based on a condition rather than a fixed count using while.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to update the condition variable, causing an infinite loop
Using while where a for loop reads more clearly
example_ideas: Infinite loop bug caused by a forgotten increment
When to reach for while over for

topics.title: Do-While Loops
topics.learning_objective: Guarantee at least one execution of a loop body using do-while.
common_mistakes: Not realizing the body runs once before the condition is ever checked
Using do-while when a plain while was clearer
example_ideas: Menu-prompt-until-valid-input example
while vs do-while executed once with a false condition

topics.title: Break and Continue
topics.learning_objective: Control loop execution flow precisely using break and continue.
common_mistakes: Using break when continue was intended, skipping too much
Overusing break to fake structured logic
example_ideas: Search-and-stop loop using break
Skip-even-numbers loop using continue

topics.title: Method Basics
topics.learning_objective: Define and call a method to organize reusable blocks of logic.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to specify a return type or using void incorrectly
Confusing calling a method with defining one
example_ideas: Extracting repeated code into one method
Method signature anatomy in one shot

topics.title: Parameters and Return Values
topics.learning_objective: Pass data into methods via parameters and get data back out via return values.
common_mistakes: Assuming Java passes objects by reference the same way as primitives
Forgetting a return statement on every code path
example_ideas: Pass-by-value proof with a primitive vs an object field mutation
Method that returns a computed value vs one that returns void

topics.title: Method Overloading
topics.learning_objective: Define multiple methods with the same name but different parameter lists.
common_mistakes: Trying to overload by return type alone
Creating ambiguous overloads the compiler can't resolve
example_ideas: Same method name, different parameter types, side by side
Ambiguous overload compiler error explained

topics.title: Variable Scope
topics.learning_objective: Understand where a variable is visible and how long it lives (local vs field scope).
common_mistakes: Shadowing a field with a local variable of the same name unintentionally
Trying to use a local variable outside its block
example_ideas: Variable shadowing bug caught live
Block scope demonstrated with a loop variable

topics.title: Static vs Instance Members
topics.learning_objective: Distinguish members that belong to the class (static) from members that belong to each object (instance).
common_mistakes: Calling an instance method from a static context without an object
Using static fields where per-object state was actually needed
example_ideas: Shared counter using a static field across instances
Why main() has to be static

topics.title: Recursion Basics
topics.learning_objective: Write a method that calls itself with a correct base case and progress toward it.
common_mistakes: Missing or unreachable base case causing a StackOverflowError
Using recursion where a simple loop was clearer and faster
example_ideas: Factorial function walked through call by call
Recursion vs loop for the same problem, side by side

topics.title: Arrays Basics
topics.learning_objective: Declare, populate, and iterate over a fixed-size array.
common_mistakes: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException from off-by-one indexing
Confusing array length (a field) with a String's length() method
example_ideas: Fill and print an array with a for loop
ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException triggered live

topics.title: Multidimensional Arrays
topics.learning_objective: Represent grid-like data using a 2D array and iterate it with nested loops.
common_mistakes: Mixing up row and column index order
Assuming all rows are the same length (jagged arrays)
example_ideas: Tic-tac-toe board as a 2D array
Nested loop printing a grid

topics.title: String Basics
topics.learning_objective: Create and use String values, and understand that String is an object, not a primitive.
common_mistakes: Using == to compare String content instead of .equals()
Assuming Strings are mutable
example_ideas: == vs .equals() on two equal-looking Strings
String literal pool explained in 10 seconds

topics.title: Common String Methods
topics.learning_objective: Use core String methods like substring, indexOf, split, and trim to manipulate text.
common_mistakes: Off-by-one errors with substring's end index
Forgetting split() returns an array, not a String
example_ideas: substring() boundary walkthrough
Splitting a CSV line into fields

topics.title: String Immutability
topics.learning_objective: Explain why String objects can't be changed in place and what that means for every 'modifying' method.
common_mistakes: Calling a String method and expecting the original variable to change
Not realizing repeated concatenation creates many intermediate objects
example_ideas: str.toUpperCase() called but not reassigned, so nothing changes
Why every String method returns a new String

topics.title: StringBuilder
topics.learning_objective: Use StringBuilder to build strings efficiently in a loop instead of repeated concatenation.
common_mistakes: Using + concatenation inside a large loop
Forgetting to call .toString() when a String is required
example_ideas: Building a large string in a loop: String + vs StringBuilder speed comparison
Chained .append() calls

topics.title: Classes and Objects
topics.learning_objective: Define a class as a blueprint and create objects (instances) from it.
common_mistakes: Confusing the class definition with an instance of it
Putting unrelated behavior into one class instead of modeling it properly
example_ideas: A Car class turned into three different Car objects
Class vs object explained with a cookie-cutter analogy, shown in code

topics.title: Constructors
topics.learning_objective: Initialize object state at creation time using constructors, including overloaded constructors.
common_mistakes: Forgetting a no-arg constructor disappears once any constructor is defined
Duplicating initialization logic across overloaded constructors instead of chaining them
example_ideas: Object created with missing vs provided constructor args
Two overloaded constructors side by side

topics.title: The 'this' Keyword
topics.learning_objective: Use 'this' to refer to the current object and disambiguate fields from parameters.
common_mistakes: Field left unset because a parameter shadowed it without using this
Using this() constructor chaining incorrectly
example_ideas: Constructor parameter shadowing a field, fixed with this.field
this() used to chain one constructor into another

topics.title: Encapsulation
topics.learning_objective: Protect object state using private fields and public getters/setters.
common_mistakes: Making every field public 'to keep things simple'
Writing getters/setters that don't actually add any validation or protection
example_ideas: Private field with a validating setter that rejects a bad value
Public field vs private-field-with-getter side by side

topics.title: Inheritance
topics.learning_objective: Share behavior between classes using extends and understand the parent-child relationship.
common_mistakes: Using inheritance where composition would model the problem better
Forgetting a subclass constructor must call the parent via super()
example_ideas: Animal parent class with Dog and Cat subclasses
super() call shown explicitly in a subclass constructor

topics.title: Method Overriding
topics.learning_objective: Override a parent method in a subclass to provide specialized behavior, using @Override correctly.
common_mistakes: Overloading instead of overriding by mismatching the method signature
Omitting @Override and missing a typo that silently creates a new method
example_ideas: Dog overrides makeSound(), Cat overrides it differently
@Override catching a typo'd method name

topics.title: Polymorphism
topics.learning_objective: Treat different subclasses through a common parent type and have the correct overridden method run at call time.
common_mistakes: Expecting the compile-time (reference) type's method to run instead of the runtime (object) type's
Not realizing an array/list of the parent type can hold mixed subclass instances
example_ideas: Animal[] array holding Dog and Cat, same call producing different sounds
Reference type vs actual object type explained visually

topics.title: Abstraction
topics.learning_objective: Understand abstraction as hiding implementation detail behind a simpler, well-defined interface.
common_mistakes: Treating abstraction and encapsulation as the same concept
Over-abstracting a simple problem before there's a second implementation to justify it
example_ideas: Driving a car without knowing the engine internals, mapped to a method call
Same method call, hidden different implementations

topics.title: Abstract Classes
topics.learning_objective: Define an abstract class with a mix of implemented and unimplemented methods that subclasses must complete.
common_mistakes: Trying to instantiate an abstract class directly
Marking a class abstract with no abstract methods, when a concrete class would do
example_ideas: Shape abstract class with an abstract area() method, implemented by Circle and Square
Compiler error from instantiating an abstract class directly

topics.title: Interfaces
topics.learning_objective: Define a contract with an interface and implement it across unrelated classes.
common_mistakes: Assuming a class can only implement one interface (it can implement many)
Confusing when to use an interface vs an abstract class
example_ideas: Flyable interface implemented by both Bird and Airplane
Interface vs abstract class decision in 10 seconds

topics.title: Access Modifiers
topics.learning_objective: Control visibility of classes, fields, and methods using public, private, protected, and package-private.
common_mistakes: Not knowing the default (package-private) access level exists when no modifier is written
Overusing public when a narrower modifier would enforce encapsulation better
example_ideas: Four access levels visualized as concentric circles
protected access demonstrated across a package boundary

topics.title: The static and final Keywords
topics.learning_objective: Use final to prevent reassignment, overriding, or subclassing, and combine it correctly with static.
common_mistakes: Believing final on an object reference makes the object itself immutable
Marking a method final prematurely, blocking legitimate future overrides
example_ideas: final variable reassignment compiler error
final object reference whose internal state can still change

topics.title: Collections Framework Overview
topics.learning_objective: Understand the List, Set, and Map interfaces as the three core shapes of the Collections Framework.
common_mistakes: Defaulting to arrays when a resizable collection was the right tool
Not knowing which top-level interface (List/Set/Map) a given class implements
example_ideas: Array's fixed size vs a collection's resizing, side by side
List vs Set vs Map: one sentence each

topics.title: ArrayList
topics.learning_objective: Use ArrayList to store a resizable, ordered collection of elements.
common_mistakes: Modifying a list while iterating it with a for-each loop
Using ArrayList when index-based access isn't actually needed
example_ideas: ConcurrentModificationException triggered live
add/remove/get walked through on one list

topics.title: LinkedList
topics.learning_objective: Compare LinkedList to ArrayList and know when its insertion/removal performance actually matters.
common_mistakes: Assuming LinkedList is always faster than ArrayList
Using get(index) heavily on a LinkedList, which is slow by design
example_ideas: ArrayList vs LinkedList insert-at-front comparison
When random access performance actually matters

topics.title: HashSet
topics.learning_objective: Store unique elements with no guaranteed order using HashSet.
common_mistakes: Expecting HashSet to preserve insertion order
Adding a custom object without correctly overriding equals()/hashCode()
example_ideas: Duplicate add() silently ignored
Custom object added twice because hashCode/equals weren't overridden

topics.title: TreeSet
topics.learning_objective: Store unique elements in sorted order using TreeSet.
common_mistakes: Adding elements with no natural ordering and no comparator, causing a runtime error
Assuming TreeSet performs like HashSet
example_ideas: Numbers added out of order, printed sorted automatically
ClassCastException from an unorderable custom type

topics.title: HashMap
topics.learning_objective: Store key-value pairs and retrieve values by key using HashMap.
common_mistakes: Using a mutable object as a key and then changing it after insertion
Calling get() on a missing key and NPE-ing on the result instead of checking for null
example_ideas: Word-frequency counter built in a few lines
getOrDefault() vs manual null-check side by side

topics.title: TreeMap
topics.learning_objective: Store key-value pairs in sorted key order using TreeMap.
common_mistakes: Reaching for TreeMap when insertion order (LinkedHashMap) was actually what was needed
Not providing a comparator for keys with no natural ordering
example_ideas: HashMap vs TreeMap iteration order side by side
Sorted leaderboard built with a TreeMap

topics.title: Queue and Deque
topics.learning_objective: Model first-in-first-out and double-ended access patterns using Queue and Deque.
common_mistakes: Confusing offer/poll (safe, return null/false) with add/remove (throw exceptions)
Using a List where a Deque better expresses stack/queue intent
example_ideas: Queue used to simulate a ticket line
Deque used as both a stack and a queue

topics.title: Iterator
topics.learning_objective: Traverse and safely remove elements from a collection using Iterator instead of a for-each loop.
common_mistakes: Calling collection.remove() during a for-each loop instead of iterator.remove()
Calling next() without checking hasNext() first
example_ideas: Safe removal during iteration using iterator.remove()
ConcurrentModificationException fixed by switching to Iterator

topics.title: Comparable vs Comparator
topics.learning_objective: Define a natural ordering with Comparable or a custom ordering with Comparator.
common_mistakes: Implementing compareTo() inconsistently with equals()
Reaching for Comparable when a one-off custom sort (Comparator) was more appropriate
example_ideas: Sorting a list of custom objects two different ways with two Comparators
compareTo() implemented for a Person class by age

topics.title: Exceptions Basics
topics.learning_objective: Understand what an exception is and how it interrupts normal program flow.
common_mistakes: Treating every error the same regardless of type
Not realizing an uncaught exception crashes the whole program
example_ideas: Uncaught NullPointerException crashing a program, shown live
Exception object as 'a description of what went wrong'

topics.title: Try, Catch, and Finally
topics.learning_objective: Handle an exception gracefully using try/catch and guarantee cleanup with finally.
common_mistakes: Catching Exception broadly instead of the specific type expected
Assuming finally won't run if the try block returns
example_ideas: Division by zero caught and handled gracefully
finally running even after a return in try

topics.title: Checked vs Unchecked Exceptions
topics.learning_objective: Distinguish exceptions the compiler forces you to handle from those it doesn't, and know why the distinction exists.
common_mistakes: Wrapping every checked exception in a try/catch that just swallows it
Treating RuntimeException subclasses as if they must be declared with throws
example_ideas: IOException (checked) vs NullPointerException (unchecked) side by side
Compiler error when a checked exception isn't handled or declared

topics.title: Custom Exceptions
topics.learning_objective: Define a custom exception class to represent a domain-specific error condition.
common_mistakes: Extending Exception vs RuntimeException without understanding the checked/unchecked consequence
Creating a custom exception with no useful message or context
example_ideas: InsufficientFundsException thrown from a bank account withdraw method
Custom exception class in five lines

topics.title: Try-With-Resources
topics.learning_objective: Automatically close resources like files and streams using try-with-resources.
common_mistakes: Manually closing resources in a finally block when try-with-resources is cleaner and safer
Using a resource type that doesn't implement AutoCloseable
example_ideas: File left open due to a missed manual close(), fixed with try-with-resources
Multiple resources closed automatically in reverse order

topics.title: File I/O Basics
topics.learning_objective: Understand the File class and the basic concept of reading from and writing to the filesystem.
common_mistakes: Assuming a File object creates the actual file on disk just by being constructed
Not handling the case where a file doesn't exist
example_ideas: File.exists() checked before an operation
Creating a File object vs actually creating the file

topics.title: Reading Files
topics.learning_objective: Read text from a file line by line using modern Java file-reading APIs.
common_mistakes: Not closing the file after reading (fixed by try-with-resources)
Reading an entire large file into memory at once unnecessarily
example_ideas: Reading a text file line by line and printing each line
FileNotFoundException handled gracefully

topics.title: Writing Files
topics.learning_objective: Write text data to a file, understanding overwrite vs append behavior.
common_mistakes: Overwriting a file unintentionally when append mode was intended
Forgetting to flush/close the writer, losing buffered data
example_ideas: Append mode vs overwrite mode on the same file, shown twice
Writing a simple log line to a file

topics.title: Buffered Streams
topics.learning_objective: Wrap raw streams in buffered versions to improve I/O performance.
common_mistakes: Reading/writing one byte or character at a time without buffering
Assuming buffering is automatic without wrapping the stream
example_ideas: Unbuffered vs buffered read performance comparison on a large file
BufferedReader wrapping a FileReader in one line

topics.title: Serialization Basics
topics.learning_objective: Convert an object to a byte stream and back using the Serializable interface.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to implement Serializable and getting a runtime exception
Not defining serialVersionUID and running into version-mismatch issues later
example_ideas: An object written to a file, then read back and printed
NotSerializableException triggered by a missing interface

topics.title: Lambda Expressions
topics.learning_objective: Write a lambda expression as a compact implementation of a single-method interface.
common_mistakes: Trying to use a lambda where the target isn't a functional interface
Overcomplicating a lambda body when a method reference would be clearer
example_ideas: Runnable implemented as an anonymous class, then rewritten as a lambda
Comparator lambda sorting a list in one line

topics.title: Functional Interfaces
topics.learning_objective: Recognize and use the built-in functional interfaces (Function, Predicate, Supplier, Consumer).
common_mistakes: Defining a custom functional interface when a built-in one already fits
Confusing Function's input/output type parameter order
example_ideas: Predicate used to filter a list
Function vs Consumer vs Supplier: one example each

topics.title: Introduction to Streams
topics.learning_objective: Process a collection declaratively using the Stream API instead of manual loops.
common_mistakes: Trying to reuse a stream after it's already been consumed
Reaching for a stream where a simple for-each loop reads more clearly
example_ideas: Filtering and printing a list with a stream vs a for loop, side by side
IllegalStateException from reusing a consumed stream

topics.title: Stream Operations: map, filter, reduce
topics.learning_objective: Transform and reduce stream data using map, filter, and reduce.
common_mistakes: Confusing map (transform) with filter (select)
Forgetting streams are lazy and nothing runs until a terminal operation is called
example_ideas: List of names filtered then mapped to uppercase in one chain
Sum of a list computed with reduce

topics.title: Collectors and collect()
topics.learning_objective: Gather stream results back into a List, Set, or Map using Collectors.
common_mistakes: Forgetting collect() is what actually triggers the stream pipeline to run
Using toList() manually via a loop instead of Collectors.toList()
example_ideas: Stream of objects collected into a Map keyed by one field
Grouping a list by a property with Collectors.groupingBy

topics.title: The Optional Class
topics.learning_objective: Represent a possibly-missing value explicitly using Optional instead of returning null.
common_mistakes: Calling .get() on an Optional without checking isPresent() first
Using Optional as a field type instead of just for return values
example_ideas: Method returning Optional<String> instead of a nullable String
orElse() vs orElseThrow() side by side

topics.title: Method References
topics.learning_objective: Replace a simple lambda with a method reference where it improves readability.
common_mistakes: Using a method reference where the lambda needed extra logic it can't express
Confusing the four kinds of method references (static, instance, particular object, constructor)
example_ideas: Lambda x -> System.out.println(x) rewritten as System.out::println
Constructor reference used to build objects from a stream

topics.title: Generics Basics
topics.learning_objective: Write a type-safe class or method using a generic type parameter.
common_mistakes: Using raw types and losing compile-time type safety
Not understanding why List<Object> isn't a substitute for a generic List<T>
example_ideas: Box<T> class holding any type safely
Raw type warning shown, then fixed with a type parameter

topics.title: Generic Methods
topics.learning_objective: Declare a method with its own type parameter, independent of the class it's in.
common_mistakes: Forgetting the type parameter declaration before the return type
Adding a class-level type parameter when only one method actually needs it
example_ideas: Generic swap(T[] array, int i, int j) method
Same generic method used with Integer and String

topics.title: Bounded Type Parameters
topics.learning_objective: Restrict a generic type parameter to a specific type or its subtypes using extends.
common_mistakes: Trying to call a method on T that only exists on the bound type, without declaring the bound
Confusing a bounded type parameter with a wildcard
example_ideas: <T extends Comparable<T>> used to write a generic max() method
Compiler error from calling compareTo() without the bound declared

topics.title: Wildcards (? extends / ? super)
topics.learning_objective: Use wildcards to write flexible methods that accept a range of generic types.
common_mistakes: Trying to add elements to a List<? extends T>
Confusing when to use extends (producer) vs super (consumer)
example_ideas: List<? extends Number> accepted by a sum method
PECS (producer-extends, consumer-super) shown with two method signatures

topics.title: Threads Basics
topics.learning_objective: Understand what a thread is and how running code concurrently differs from running it sequentially.
common_mistakes: Assuming thread execution order is predictable
Calling run() directly instead of start(), which just runs it on the current thread
example_ideas: Two threads printing interleaved output unpredictably
start() vs run() side by side

topics.title: Runnable vs Extending Thread
topics.learning_objective: Implement Runnable instead of extending Thread to keep class design flexible.
common_mistakes: Extending Thread unnecessarily and losing the ability to extend anything else
Not realizing Runnable is just a task, and still needs a Thread to run it
example_ideas: Same task implemented as a Runnable vs a Thread subclass
Runnable passed to a Thread constructor

topics.title: Synchronization
topics.learning_objective: Protect shared mutable state from race conditions using the synchronized keyword.
common_mistakes: Synchronizing on the wrong object, providing no real protection
Over-synchronizing and creating unnecessary bottlenecks
example_ideas: Race condition on a shared counter shown live, then fixed with synchronized
Two threads incrementing an unsynchronized counter losing updates

topics.title: ExecutorService
topics.learning_objective: Manage a pool of threads for running tasks using ExecutorService instead of creating threads manually.
common_mistakes: Creating a new Thread per task instead of reusing a pool
Forgetting to shut down the ExecutorService, leaking threads
example_ideas: Manual thread creation vs a fixed thread pool running the same tasks
submit() returning a Future, then calling .get()

topics.title: Concurrent Collections
topics.learning_objective: Use thread-safe collections like ConcurrentHashMap instead of manually synchronizing a regular collection.
common_mistakes: Manually synchronizing every access to a HashMap instead of using ConcurrentHashMap
Assuming all concurrent collection operations are still atomic in combination (e.g. check-then-act)
example_ideas: HashMap shared across threads causing corruption, fixed with ConcurrentHashMap
putIfAbsent() used to avoid a check-then-act race

topics.title: JVM Architecture
topics.learning_objective: Understand the JVM's major components: class loader, runtime data areas, and execution engine.
common_mistakes: Treating the JVM as a black box with no mental model at all
Confusing the heap and the stack's purposes
example_ideas: Diagram of class loader -> memory areas -> execution engine
Heap vs stack: where does each piece of data live

topics.title: Garbage Collection
topics.learning_objective: Understand how the JVM automatically reclaims memory from objects that are no longer reachable.
common_mistakes: Believing System.gc() forces immediate collection
Thinking Java has no memory leaks because it's garbage collected
example_ideas: Object made unreachable, eligible for collection, shown conceptually
How a lingering reference in a static collection causes a 'Java memory leak'

topics.title: Memory Management: Stack vs Heap
topics.learning_objective: Explain what lives on the stack vs the heap and why that distinction affects performance and lifetime.
common_mistakes: Assuming all variables live in the same place
Confusing a stack overflow (too much recursion) with a heap OutOfMemoryError
example_ideas: Local primitive on the stack vs an object on the heap, traced through one method call
StackOverflowError vs OutOfMemoryError: what causes each

topics.title: The JIT Compiler
topics.learning_objective: Understand how the Just-In-Time compiler optimizes bytecode into native machine code at runtime.
common_mistakes: Assuming Java is 'purely interpreted' and therefore always slow
Not realizing 'warm-up time' affects benchmark results
example_ideas: Bytecode interpreted first, then JIT-compiled after enough executions
Why a benchmark's first run is often slower than later runs

topics.title: Practical Performance Tips
topics.learning_objective: Apply a handful of well-known, low-risk practices that meaningfully improve everyday Java performance.
common_mistakes: Micro-optimizing code that isn't actually a bottleneck
Avoiding standard library collections in favor of hand-rolled ones for no measured reason
example_ideas: StringBuilder in a loop vs String concatenation, revisited with JVM context
Right-sizing an ArrayList/HashMap upfront to avoid resize overhead

topics.title: OOP Interview Questions
topics.learning_objective: Answer common interview questions that test OOP understanding (overriding vs overloading, abstract class vs interface, etc.).
common_mistakes: Reciting a definition without being able to give a concrete example
Not being able to explain 'why' a design choice matters, only 'what' it is
example_ideas: 'What's the difference between overloading and overriding?' answered in 15 seconds
'Abstract class or interface?' decision framework

topics.title: Collections Interview Questions
topics.learning_objective: Answer common interview questions about collection internals and tradeoffs (HashMap vs TreeMap, ArrayList vs LinkedList, etc.).
common_mistakes: Not knowing the rough time complexity of common collection operations
Confusing which collections allow duplicates or null values
example_ideas: 'When would you use a TreeMap over a HashMap?' answered concretely
Time complexity cheat-sheet for the most common collections

topics.title: Multithreading Interview Questions
topics.learning_objective: Answer common interview questions about threads, race conditions, and synchronization.
common_mistakes: Describing a race condition vaguely instead of with a concrete example
Not being able to explain what synchronized actually locks
example_ideas: 'What is a race condition?' with a two-thread counter example
'What does synchronized actually do?' in one clear sentence

topics.title: Common Coding Patterns for Interviews
topics.learning_objective: Recognize recurring problem-solving patterns (two-pointer, sliding window, frequency map) as applied in Java.
common_mistakes: Memorizing a solution instead of recognizing the underlying pattern
